# react-ant-resource-list
> react-ant-resource-list is a comprehensive React component library for building Ant Design-based resource list interfaces. It provides a production-ready solution for creating standardized data management interfaces with built-in CRUD operations, pagination, and action buttons.

All components are written in TypeScript and built on top of Ant Design 5.x and @jswork/antd-components. The library simplifies the creation of resource list pages with consistent UI patterns and reduced boilerplate code.

**Important notes for agents:**
- react-ant-resource-list wraps AcCard and AcTable from @jswork/antd-components
- Main component is `ReactAntResourceList` - a class-based component
- Integrates with `@jswork/next` (nx) for API handling via the `nx.$api` pattern
- API methods follow naming convention: `{name}_index` (e.g., `posts_index`)
- Expected API response format: `{ rows: [], total: number }`
- Built-in action system with add, reset, and back buttons
- Supports i18n with default language: zh-CN
- Component is located at `./packages/lib/src/`
- The library is actively maintained with regular updates

## Documentation
- [Homepage](https://js.work): Official project homepage
- [Live Preview](https://afeiship.github.io/react-ant-resource-list/): Interactive component showcase
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/afeiship/react-ant-resource-list): Source code and issues
- [NPM Package](https://www.npmjs.org/package/@jswork/react-ant-resource-list): Package information

## Key Features
- **All-in-One Resource List:** Combines Card, Table, and Action buttons in a single component
- **API Integration:** Seamless integration with nx.$api pattern for data fetching
- **Built-in Actions:** Pre-configured action buttons (add, reset, back) using AcCardExtras
- **Pagination Support:** Automatic pagination handling with customizable page sizes
- **Action Column:** Optional action column for row-level operations (edit, delete)
- **i18n Ready:** Built-in internationalization support
- **TypeScript Support:** Full type definitions with exported prop types
- **Flexible Configuration:** Extensive props for customization
- **Responsive Design:** Mobile-friendly layout based on Ant Design 5.x

## Installation

### Basic Installation
```bash
npm install -S @jswork/react-ant-resource-list
```

### Dependencies
The library requires these peer dependencies:
- `antd` (Ant Design 5.x)
- `@ant-design/icons`
- `@jswork/antd-components`
- `@jswork/next`
- `classnames`
- `react`
- `react-dom`

### Setup

1. **Import Styles:**
```scss
// CSS
@import "~@jswork/react-ant-resource-list/dist/style.css";

// or SCSS
@import "~@jswork/react-ant-resource-list/dist/style.scss";
```

2. **Configure API:**
```js
import nx from '@jswork/next';

// Set up API endpoints (follows {name}_index convention)
nx.set(nx, '$api', {
  posts_index: async (payload) => {
    // payload contains: { page, size, ...params }
    const res = await fetch(`/api/posts?page=${payload.page}&limit=${payload.size}`);
    return {
      rows: res,      // Array of data items
      total: 16,     // Total count for pagination
    };
  },
});
```

3. **Import and Use Component:**
```tsx
import ReactAntResourceList from '@jswork/react-ant-resource-list';
import '@jswork/react-ant-resource-list/dist/style.scss';

function App() {
  const columns = [
    { title: 'ID', dataIndex: 'id', width: 80 },
    { title: 'Title', dataIndex: 'title', width: 200 },
    { title: 'Content', dataIndex: 'content', width: 200 },
  ];

  return (
    <ReactAntResourceList
      title="文章列表"
      name="posts"
      columns={columns}
    />
  );
}
```

## Component Reference

### `ReactAntResourceList`

The main component that provides a complete resource list interface with card wrapper, table, and action buttons.

#### Basic Usage
```tsx
import ReactAntResourceList from '@jswork/react-ant-resource-list';

<ReactAntResourceList
  title="文章列表"
  name="posts"
  columns={[
    { title: 'ID', dataIndex: 'id', width: 80 },
    { title: 'Title', dataIndex: 'title', width: 200 },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | `string` | **required** | Resource identifier (used for API method `{name}_index`) |
| `columns` | `TableProps['columns']` | `[]` | Table column definitions |
| `columnsFields` | `TableProps['columns']` | `undefined` | Additional field columns for the table |
| `columnsAction` | `AcTableProps['columns']` | `undefined` | Action column configuration (edit, delete, etc.) |
| `columnsActionParams` | `Record<string, any>` | `undefined` | Additional parameters for action column |
| `title` | `string` | `undefined` | Card title |
| `lang` | `string` | `'zh-CN'` | Language setting for i18n |
| `module` | `string` | `'admin'` | Module namespace for API routing |
| `hasBack` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether to show back button in actions |
| `header` | `ReactNode` | `undefined` | Custom header content above table |
| `footer` | `ReactNode` | `undefined` | Custom footer content below table |
| `params` | `any` | `undefined` | Additional query parameters for API request |
| `paramsAdd` | `any` | `undefined` | Parameters for add action (used in navigation/form) |
| `paramsEdit` | `any` | `undefined` | Parameters for edit action (used in navigation/form) |
| `cardExtraProps` | `Omit<AcCardExtrasProps, 'name' \| 'lang'>` | `{actions: ['add', 'reset']}` | Configuration for card action buttons |
| `tableProps` | `Omit<AcTableProps, ...>` | `undefined` | Additional props passed to AcTable component |
| `className` | `string` | `undefined` | Additional CSS class names |
| ...rest | `CardProps` | `undefined` | Any other props passed to Ant Design Card |

#### Detailed Prop Explanations

**`name`** (required)
- Resource identifier used to construct API method name
- API method follows pattern: `{name}_index`
- Example: `name="posts"` → calls `nx.$api.posts_index(payload)`

**`columns`**
- Standard Ant Design table column definitions
- Array of column configuration objects
- Example:
```tsx
columns={[
  { title: 'ID', dataIndex: 'id', width: 80 },
  { title: 'Name', dataIndex: 'name', width: 200 },
  { title: 'Email', dataIndex: 'email', width: 250 },
]}
```

**`columnsAction`**
- Configuration for action column in each row
- Typically contains edit and delete buttons
- Example:
```tsx
columnsAction={[
  {
    title: 'Actions',
    key: 'actions',
    render: (_, record) => (
      <Space>
        <Button onClick={() => handleEdit(record)}>Edit</Button>
        <Button onClick={() => handleDelete(record)}>Delete</Button>
      </Space>
    ),
  }
]}
```

**`cardExtraProps`**
- Controls the action buttons displayed in card header
- Default actions: `['add', 'reset']`
- Set `hasBack=true` to include back button
- Example:
```tsx
cardExtraProps={{
  actions: ['add', 'reset'],  // Available actions
  // Other AcCardExtras props...
}}
```

**`tableProps`**
- Additional props passed to the underlying AcTable component
- Can override table size, border style, pagination, etc.
- Example:
```tsx
tableProps={{
  size: 'small',
  showHeader: true,
  pagination: { pageSize: 20 },
}}
```

**`params`**
- Additional parameters passed to API request
- Merged with default `{page, size}` payload
- Example:
```tsx
params={{ status: 'active', category: 'news' }}
// API receives: { page: 1, size: 10, status: 'active', category: 'news' }
```

#### Action Buttons

The component includes built-in action buttons:
- **Add**: Navigates to create page (configured via `paramsAdd`)
- **Reset**: Refreshes the list data
- **Back**: Returns to previous page (when `hasBack=true`)

## Usage Examples

### Basic Resource List
```tsx
import ReactAntResourceList from '@jswork/react-ant-resource-list';
import '@jswork/react-ant-resource-list/dist/style.scss';
import nx from '@jswork/next';

// Setup API
nx.set(nx, '$api', {
  posts_index: async (payload) => {
    const res = await fetch(
      `https://api.example.com/posts?page=${payload.page}&limit=${payload.size}`
    ).then(r => r.json());
    return { rows: res.data, total: res.total };
  },
});

function PostList() {
  const columns = [
    { title: 'ID', dataIndex: 'id', width: 80 },
    { title: 'Title', dataIndex: 'title', width: 200 },
    { title: 'Author', dataIndex: 'author', width: 150 },
  ];

  return (
    <ReactAntResourceList
      title="文章列表"
      name="posts"
      columns={columns}
    />
  );
}
```

### With Custom Actions and Parameters
```tsx
function EnhancedPostList() {
  const columns = [
    { title: 'ID', dataIndex: 'id', width: 80 },
    { title: 'Title', dataIndex: 'title', width: 200 },
  ];

  const columnsAction = [
    {
      title: '操作',
      key: 'actions',
      render: (_, record) => (
        <Space>
          <a onClick={() => navigate(`/posts/edit/${record.id}`)}>Edit</a>
          <a onClick={() => handleDelete(record.id)}>Delete</a>
        </Space>
      ),
    },
  ];

  return (
    <ReactAntResourceList
      title="文章管理"
      name="posts"
      columns={columns}
      columnsAction={columnsAction}
      params={{ status: 'published' }}
      paramsAdd={{ from: 'list' }}
      paramsEdit={{ mode: 'full' }}
      hasBack={true}
      lang="zh-CN"
    />
  );
}
```

### With Custom Header and Footer
```tsx
function CustomPostList() {
  const columns = [
    { title: 'ID', dataIndex: 'id' },
    { title: 'Title', dataIndex: 'title' },
  ];

  return (
    <ReactAntResourceList
      title="文章列表"
      name="posts"
      columns={columns}
      header={
        <div className="mb-4">
          <Alert message="Tips: 点击行可查看详情" type="info" />
        </div>
      }
      footer={
        <div className="mt-4 text-center text-gray-500">
          Total items loaded from server
        </div>
      }
    />
  );
}
```

### With Multiple Column Types
```tsx
function MultiColumnList() {
  // Basic data columns
  const columns = [
    { title: 'ID', dataIndex: 'id', width: 80 },
    { title: 'Name', dataIndex: 'name', width: 150 },
  ];

  // Additional field columns
  const columnsFields = [
    { title: 'Email', dataIndex: 'email', width: 200 },
    { title: 'Phone', dataIndex: 'phone', width: 150 },
  ];

  // Action column
  const columnsAction = [
    {
      title: 'Actions',
      key: 'actions',
      render: (_, record) => (
        <Button type="link" onClick={() => console.log(record)}>
          View
        </Button>
      ),
    },
  ];

  return (
    <ReactAntResourceList
      title="用户列表"
      name="users"
      columns={columns}
      columnsFields={columnsFields}
      columnsAction={columnsAction}
      module="admin"
    />
  );
}
```

### With Custom Table Configuration
```tsx
function CustomTableList() {
  const columns = [
    { title: 'ID', dataIndex: 'id' },
    { title: 'Title', dataIndex: 'title' },
  ];

  return (
    <ReactAntResourceList
      title="文章列表"
      name="posts"
      columns={columns}
      tableProps={{
        size: 'small',
        bordered: true,
        showHeader: true,
        pagination: {
          pageSize: 20,
          showSizeChanger: true,
          showQuickJumper: true,
        },
      }}
    />
  );
}
```

## API Integration Pattern

### Standard API Response Format
The component expects API responses in this format:
```typescript
{
  rows: Array<any>;   // Data array
  total: number;      // Total count for pagination
}
```

### API Payload Structure
The component sends this payload to the API:
```typescript
{
  page: number;       // Current page number
  size: number;       // Page size
  ...params          // Additional params from props
}
```

### Complete API Setup Example
```typescript
import nx from '@jswork/next';

interface ApiPayload {
  page: number;
  size: number;
  [key: string]: any;
}

interface ApiResponse {
  rows: any[];
  total: number;
}

nx.set(nx, '$api', {
  // List endpoint
  posts_index: async (payload: ApiPayload): Promise<ApiResponse> => {
    const { page, size, ...filters } = payload;
    const params = new URLSearchParams({
      page: page.toString(),
      limit: size.toString(),
      ...filters,
    });

    const response = await fetch(`/api/posts?${params}`);
    const data = await response.json();

    return {
      rows: data.items,
      total: data.totalCount,
    };
  },

  // You can define other CRUD operations too
  posts_create: async (payload) => { /* ... */ },
  posts_update: async (payload) => { /* ... */ },
  posts_delete: async (payload) => { /* ... */ },
});
```

## Advanced Usage

### i18n Support
```tsx
// Default language is zh-CN
<ReactAntResourceList name="posts" columns={columns} lang="zh-CN" />

// English (requires proper i18n setup)
<ReactAntResourceList name="posts" columns={columns} lang="en-US" />
```

### Module Namespace
```tsx
// Default module is 'admin'
// This affects API routing/naming
<ReactAntResourceList
  name="posts"
  module="admin"
  columns={columns}
/>

// Custom module
<ReactAntResourceList
  name="posts"
  module="user"
  columns={columns}
/>
```

### Custom Styling
```tsx
// Add custom class name
<ReactAntResourceList
  name="posts"
  columns={columns}
  className="my-custom-resource-list"
/>

// CSS
.my-custom-resource-list {
  margin: 20px;
  border-radius: 8px;
}
```

## Usage Notes for Agents

### Component Type
- `ReactAntResourceList` is a **class-based component**
- Extends React.Component
- Display name: `'react-ant-resource-list'`
- Version: dynamically injected via `__VERSION__` at build time

### Naming Convention
- API methods follow pattern: `{name}_{action}`
- List endpoint: `{name}_index`
- Example: `posts_index`, `users_index`, `products_index`

### Import Patterns
```tsx
// Default import (recommended)
import ReactAntResourceList from '@jswork/react-ant-resource-list';

// Import types
import type { ReactAntResourceListProps } from '@jswork/react-ant-resource-list';
```

### Component Dependencies
The component internally uses:
- `antd` - Card component
- `@jswork/antd-components` - AcCardExtras, AcTable
- `classnames` - CSS class manipulation
- `@jswork/next` - nx utility for API

### State Management
- Component manages its own internal state for pagination
- Data fetching is handled via `nx.$api` pattern
- No external state management required

### Event Handling
- Action button clicks trigger navigation via `nx.$event`
- Pagination changes trigger data refetch
- Row actions (edit/delete) are configured via `columnsAction`

### Development
```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Build library
npm run build

# Run example
npm run dev:example

# Publish release
npm run release
```

### Monorepo Structure
```
react-ant-resource-list/
├── packages/
│   ├── lib/          # Main library package
│   └── example/      # Demo/example application
├── lerna.json        # Lerna configuration
└── package.json      # Root workspace
```

## Common Patterns

### CRUD Operation Flow
1. **List**: Component automatically calls `{name}_index` on mount
2. **Add**: Click "Add" button → navigate to create page (uses `paramsAdd`)
3. **Edit**: Click edit in row → navigate to edit page (uses `paramsEdit`)
4. **Delete**: Click delete in row → handle via `columnsAction`

### Pagination Flow
1. User changes page/pageSize
2. Component sends new payload with updated `{page, size}`
3. API returns data for that page
4. Table updates with new data

### Search/Filter Flow
1. User filters data (via separate search form)
2. Update `params` prop with filter criteria
3. Component sends new payload including filters
4. API returns filtered results
